summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Composition & Layers of the Atmosphere
summary_sections.content_html:
Earth's atmosphere is a thin layer of gases essential for life:

  • Composition: 78% N₂, 21% O₂, ~1% Ar, ~0.04% CO₂, plus water vapor and trace gases
  • Layers (from surface upward):
    • Troposphere (0–12 km): weather occurs here; temperature decreases with altitude
    • Stratosphere (12–50 km): contains the ozone layer; temperature increases
    • Mesosphere (50–80 km): coldest layer; meteors burn up here
    • Thermosphere (80–700 km): very hot but thin; auroras occur here; includes the ionosphere
    • Exosphere (>700 km): outermost layer; transitions into space
